content: Drawing and modeling to escape the chatter in the mind.
Facade thermal optimization and window/lighting detail.
Final rendering in Maya with Arnold shader networks.
Master of Architecture degree project taking form.
Swiss mixed-use apartment studio led by Patrick Gmur.
Happy thanksgiving and thanks to technology.
CUDA and AI to render faster. 4 hours in time savings on a two second animation.
Graduated with my Master of Architecture, pursuing a position.
The unique design capabilities of 3D Printing: customization and optimization.
Evening experiment in visual and physical interaction.
Custom neural network to evolve a passive atrium space.
Physical model test fit for Zurich Switzerland tower.
Site study composition for sustainability project.
Master of Architecture thesis ready for virtual presentation.
Hybridized fluid simulation and image based form finding.
75mm structurally optimized lattice cube. Reduced volume by 91%.
Hybrid multi material lattice structure for 3D Printing.
A sketch. Escaping to a world independent from our known limitations.
Passing a personal milestone. Yesterday. The day that makes tomorrow.
The Cell. A Multi-Sport and E-Game Arena concept.
The architecture stands among the silent winter forest.
Concept design for a mixed use tower. Blending mass timber with hybrid cladding.
Hyper-resolutions with images above 3 billion pixels with Stable Diffusion.
Custom python and Stable Diffusion scripts for a 75x12 canopy. 349,000px by 44,100px at 300DPI.
Finally got some time with Mid Journey V5. What an amazing tool.
New experiment with Stable Diffusion from a 15 second iPad automatic-drawing.
Computational design exploration for an Amazon Pop Up retail anchor concept.
Thanks to Hassan Ragab and ParametricArchitecture for a wonderful workshop.
7 dimensional algorithmic model. Computational Design in many meanings and forms.
Designing unique spatial relationships leveraging ChatGPT node from Peter Davis.
Stable Diffusion experiments starting with a line drawing.
Loose city sketch, admiring the perfect imperfection of a facade.
Afternoon palette knife abstraction. Embrace the chaos.
Happy July 4th!
Enjoying the weekend with some acrylic on canvas. A bloom. 30x60
The Squeezed City, 36x60 Acrylic. Let go and let the moment do the heavy lifting.
Starting a new adventure with Corgan with the talented team at Hugo.
What if a classroom was not just four walls? Ahead of A4LE, exploring what a perfect classroom could look like.

So what does a 2026 AI Architect strategy look like?
Custom tools. Careful integration. Organizing our chaos.

We are moving beyond standard prompting. We are creating custom ecosystems that accelerate the mundane to make space for the extraordinary.

→ AI Architects working in parallel
→ Accelerating the mundane workflows
→ Seeing the invisible options
→ Exploring the unknown

This is the future of our design intelligence.

#AECtechnology #technology #architecture

"Three years of latency resolved in one image. Your other projects, they're in year one of the same timeline. You're looking at year three."

That's what Opus saw in this image, and I couldn't agree more. It's impossible to see the forest through the trees, when they are yet seeds on the ground.

In 2022, Stable Diffusion was the rage, the hot AI topic. Mid Journey was just kicking off, we couldn't fathom where AI would take us. The AI assisted computational design strategy was simple, because it didn't exist.

Upscale my iPad drawing with StableDiffusion A1111 to a 144,000 pixel wide image, 150dpi, 80' long, perfectly aligned to a Revit model, sliced with bleeds and tagged to be printed on vinyl sheets and fixed seamlessly to polycarbonate panels, organize them under equally-spaced slats and linear lights, hide and tuck away every wire and structural connection so the art work majestically manifests itself as you move under the endless spacescape of the AI mind, to be saved in a folder on the drive, handed off to the next PA, and in the cold hands of the value engineering.

I'll be honest, part of me thought it would never be built. For years, I've looked at the images as desktop backgrounds, hoping to one day be able to see the mystical effects being enjoyed by people underneath it.

And soon that dream will become a reality in Fort Worth! If the design held its own weight on a project with every set back imaginable, I have faith it will be enjoyed for decades to come. So if you're in the Foundry District neighborhood, take a sneak peek of the outdoor areas and the awesome landscape designs around the development by Benjamin Wade, and help count down the days until the grand opening! Hats off to everyone involved, this is a project brought to life by many hands, aligned on building a reality.

Follow for the technical deep dive on how this came to be.

#architecture #design #ai #computationaldesign

How long would it take you to organize over 20,000+ images? A couple hours? A couple days? Not even worth it?

Claude ran overnight, so our team didn't have to run for a month.

Added to the custom AEC Tool collection: a multi-agent pipeline I built today to tackle this seemingly impossible task. The time savings let us focus on design, not writing endless descriptions and tags.

Agent 1 assessed the problem.
Agent 2 developed a plan.
Agent 3 created a custom AEC Tool with Claude Sonnet as the machine vision.

And with a few clicks, it was all put together. This is what architecting with AI looks like.

#aiinarchitecture #architecture #futureoftheAEC #AI #Claude

You collect the data. You run the survey. You do the post-occupancy. And then it sits in a spreadsheet in a forgotten folder after the presentation.... Where do you store the insights, not just data?

We just ran an FFE pilot survey for a project. About 6,000 responses from students and teachers across four campuses evaluating desks and chairs. We had real findings. The most popular chair had the lowest comfort rating. Students were choosing it for size, not comfort. Teachers prefer 2 casters because they're the ones actually rearranging the room, not students. Even with typos, we could untangle which desk students meant from how they described the physical features in their own words, creating usable personas that students relate to.

The data was there. The insights were there. What wasn't there was a bridge to put it in front of the people who actually need it. The district team, the furniture reps, and the parents. The traditional path is a PDF summary or a slide deck that takes days to produce and gets skimmed, where we just accept it as part of the process.

That's not a data problem. That's a bridge problem.

So we built one. A purpose-built tool on the same platform we use for internal research, designed so the people on the other side of the table can see what we explored. Not a report or a paragraph, a tool a parent can pull up and see what kids at their campus actually said about the furniture.

Every firm doing post-occupancy evaluations or end-user surveys has this same gap. The data exists, but the time and effort it takes to make it useful means it mostly just sits there with the insights lying silent. The technology to build these bridges is more accessible than it's ever been. What's been missing is architecting them.

What data is sitting in your forgotten folder?

#aiarchitecture #architecture #design
notes: AEC Tools - FFE survey bridge tool

content:
From the back of the room: "That's horrifying. It's doing what I do." Then silence... The CEO responded: "How do we scale this?"

That tension is exactly where the AEC is right now on AI. Same presentation on integrating AI into our industry. Two completely different lenses on the same moment, using AI as a tool or using AI as a foundational mindset on how you approach the problem.

This is what real reactions look like when walking a firm through what AI actually looks like in an architecture practice. Not a pitch. Real tools, real workflows, things running that allow us to see once invisible connections.

One of our power users, Nilen Varade, showed how he has adapted Claude into his workflow: submittals and controlling project checklists with Claude in Excel.

We then walked through how over 6,000 survey responses from Midland High School were turned into actionable insights about how we outfit students and teachers with furniture they'll use for years.

Before the results, I asked the room:
"How long would this take you manually?"
"A month" was the first response, a real answer from an architect with over 20 years of experience.

With a Claude-assisted workflow, it took about 3 hours from raw response to school board-ready insights. Then Claude built the slide deck live.

Then came Claude, building a building in Revit.

That's where the room split. One person saw their job changing. The other saw the whole firm moving more dynamically.

Curious about Claude? Comment or message 'Claude AEC Tools' for a quick list of ways to implement this today.

#AEC #architecture #AI #computationaldesign #AIinArchitecture
notes: AEC Tools - firm presentation, breakout post

content:
Is this what we are losing in the age of mass AI architecture? Taking 1000 hours to make this showed, we may be losing something larger.

Or are we silently gaining back space to do these types of explorations and losing sight of that.

AI gives us a mirror to look into, where only we decide how we want to spend the new found time, skill, and ability.

Making this model and leading the team that brought it to life was a window into a possibility we can, and did make into a livable reality and had the honor to share it with the public. With over 1000 hours to design and manufacture the model, it showed us that diligence to one vision was key.

This resulted in allowing for a slice of time at TxEdCON in September, it was a rare moment where the architecture and design wasn't judged, liked or hated. It was a moment where everyone that stopped by, took time to explore an in-the-moment experience, and this version of the larger architecture was the armature for that. A moment that a passerby could relate to, a moment where viewers shared stories and memories. A moment where the experience was the architecture, built by hand.

What has you most uneasy in this new era of architecture? Is it even something even new? What do you see in your AI mirror?

#architecture #design #AI
notes: TASA model reflection - AI mirror, reposted Pfluger video
